hadiths.chapter: 16. CHAPTER ON RARE CASES OF LEGAL PUNISHMENTS
hadiths.ar:
5144   -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ ر ِي َّاذِ ن ْن ِ مَّزْوَّإن َّ إَّلْق َّن ْذِى ِّ عَّم َّن ْ ذ َّكَّزَّةُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال  «
سَّن َّه ِ إَّلْمَّحْق ِ ق ِى  س َّى ْءٍ ي ُّو ْكَّلُّ مِي ْلِ إَّلْح ُّن ْز ِ وَّ إَّلل َّحْمِ وَّ إَّلْق ِي  َّاءِ
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace be upon him and his family) said:  
"It is not permissible for a ruler who believes in Allah {SWT} and the Last Day to administer 
more than ten lashes except in the case of a legal punishment (ha dd)." 
[AL SADUQ] 
It is permitted to discipline a servant with three to five lashes. However, whoever 
strikes his servant beyond what is due without a valid reason has no expiation except 
to free him. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.73  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 16, H. 5143

hadiths.chapter: 16. CHAPTER ON RARE CASES OF LEGAL PUNISHMENTS
hadiths.ar:
5145  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ إ ذَّمَّ ن ْن ِ إ ِسْحَّاق َّ عَّن ْ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ إَّلْح ُّعْق ِى ِّ ق َّالَّ: كُّن ْث ُّ عِن ْذَّ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ ع و  ج َّاءَّةُّ كِي َّاب ُّ 
هِس َّامِ ن ْن ِ عَّن ْذِ إَّلْمَّلِك ِ ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ ن َّن َّس َّ إِمْزَّإ َّة ً ق َّسَّلَّن َّهَّا ي ِي َّان َّهَّا وَّ ن َّكَّحَّهَّا ق َّا ِن  َّ إَّلن  َّ اسَّ ق َّذِ إِج ْي َّلَّق ُّوإ عَّلَّي ْن َّا هَّاهُّن َّا طَّان ِق َّه   ق َّالُّوإ 
إُّق ْي ُّلُّوةُّ وَّ طَّان ِق َّه   ق َّالُّوإ إ َّجْزِق ُّوةُّ ق َّكَّي َّث َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ ِلَّي ْهِ لْحَّى ِّ جَّذ ُّةُّ إ َّن ْ ي ُّق ْطَّغَّ ي َّذُّةُّ لِن َّن ْس ِهِ 
وَّ سَّلْن ِهِ إَّلي ِّي َّاب َّ وَّ ي ُّق َّامَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلْحَّذ ُّ ق ِى  إَّلز ِّن َّا إ ِن ْ إ ُّجْصِن َّ رُّج ِمَّ وَّ إ ِن ْ لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ إ ُّجْصِن َّ ج ُّلِذَّ مِان َّه ً
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Ziyad ibn Marwan Al -Qandi from someone who reported 
from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, he said: "The hand of a thief is not to be cut off during a year of 
famine for stealing anything edible, such as bread, meat, or cucumbers." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.73  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 16, H. 5144

hadiths.chapter: 16. CHAPTER ON RARE CASES OF LEGAL PUNISHMENTS
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Adam ibn Ishaq from Abdullah ibn Muhammad Al -Jufi 
who said: I was with Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} when a letter from 
Hisham ibn Abd Al -Malik arrived regarding a man who had exhumed the grave of a 
woman, stolen her clothes, and committed intercourse with her.  
The people were divided on how to punish him: some said: “Kill him," while others 
said: “Burn him." 
Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} wrote back to him:  
"The sanctity of the deceased is like the sanctity of the living. His punishment is that 
his hand should be cut off for exhuming the grave and stealing the clothing, and the 
legal punishment for adultery is to be applied to him.  
If he was married ( muhsan), he is to be stoned, and if he was not married, he is to be 
flogged one hundred lashes." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.228 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.74 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.62 
• Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol. 10 p.116 • Al -Istibsar, Vol.4 p.246 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 15 p.353 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, 
Vol.28 p.278  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 16, H. 5145

hadiths.chapter: 16. CHAPTER ON RARE CASES OF LEGAL PUNISHMENTS
hadiths.ar:
5147  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ إَّلس َّكُّون ِى ِّ عَّن ْ ج َّعْق َّزِ ن ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم س َّلاَّمُّ إ ُّي ِى َّ ن ِس َّارِب ٍ  
ق َّاسْي َّق ْزَّإ َّةُّ إَّلْق ُّزْإ ن َّ ق َّق َّزَّإ َّةُّ ق َّا َّج َّذ َّ رِذَّإءَّةُّ ق َّا َّلْق َّاةُّ مَّغَّ إ َّرْذِي َّه ِ إَّلن  َّاسِ ي ُّم َّ ق َّالَّ لَّه
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace be upon him and his family) said:  
“Avert prescribed punishments through doubts. There is no intercession, no 
guarantee, and no oath in matters of prescribed punishments.”  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 4 p.74 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol. 28 p.47 • Al -Fusul Al -
Muhimmah, Vol.2 p.513  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 16, H. 5146

hadiths.chapter: 16. CHAPTER ON RARE CASES OF LEGAL PUNISHMENTS
hadiths.ar:
5148   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن ُّو إ َّي  ُّوب َّ عَّن ِ إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى ِّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ 
كَّان َّ ي َّص ْزِب ُّ ن ِالس َّوْطِ وَّ ن ِن ِصْف ِ إَّلس َّوْطِ وَّ ن ِن َّعْص ِهِ ي َّعْن ِى  ق ِى  إَّلْحُّذُّوذِ إ ِذ َّإ إ ُّي ِى َّ  ن ِع ُّلاَّمٍ إ َّوْ ج َّارِي َّه ٍ لَّمْ ي ُّذْرِكَّا وَّ لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ 
ي ُّن ْطِلُّ جَّذ إً مِن ْ جُّذُّوذِ إَّلل َّهِ  مِن ْ وَّسَّطِهِ ق َّي َّص ْزِب ُّ  
ن ِهِ إ َّوْ مِن ْ ي ُّلُّي ِهِ ق َّي َّص ْزِب ُّ ن ِهِ عَّلَّى ق َّ ذْرِ إ َّسْن َّان ِهِمْ كَّذ َّلِك َّ ي َّص ْزِن ُّهُّمْ ن ِالس َّوْطِ وَّ لأَّ ي ُّن ْطِلُّ جَّذ إً مِن ْ جُّذُّوذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Al -Sakuni from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}, it is reported:  
"Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} was presented with a man who had consumed alcohol.  
Imam {a.s} asked him to recite the Qur'an, and the man recited it.  
Then Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} took his cloak and threw it among the cloaks of the 
people and said to him, 'Retrieve your cloak.'  
When he could not distinguish it, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} carried out the legal 
punishment on him." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.74  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 16, H. 5147

hadiths.chapter: 16. CHAPTER ON RARE CASES OF LEGAL PUNISHMENTS
hadiths.ar:
5149  -  وَّ ج َّطَّث َّ إ َّمِي زُّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إَّلن  َّاسَّ ق َّق َّال  ق َّلاَّ ي َّعْي َّذُّوهَّا وَّ 
ق َّزَّض َّ ق َّزَّإن ِص َّ ق َّلاَّ ي َّن ْق ُّصُّوهَّا وَّ سَّكَّث َّ عَّن ْ إ َّس ْي َّاءَّ لَّمْ ي َّسْكُّث ْ عَّن ْهَّا ن ِسْي َّان اً لَّهَّا ق َّلاَّ  ي ُّكَّل َّق ُّوهَّا رَّجْمَّه ً مِن َّ إَّلل َّهِ لَّكُّمْ 
ق َّاق ْن َّلُّوهَّا  ي َّزَّك َّ مَّا إِس ْي َّن َّهَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ مِن َّ 
إَّلْأ ِي ْمِ ق َّهُّوَّ لِمَّا إِسْي َّن َّان َّ لَّهُّ إ َّي ْزَّك ُّ  وَّ إَّلْمَّعَّاصِى  جِمَّى إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ق َّمَّن ْ ي َّزْي َّغْ جَّوْلَّهَّا ي ُّوس ِك ْ إ َّن ْ ي َّذْج ُّلَّهَّا
hadiths.en:
Abu Ayyub narrated from Al-Halabi from Abu Abdullah {a.s} who said:  
"In the book of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}, it is mentioned that He {a.s} would administer 
punishment using a whip, half a whip, or even a part of it - meaning in the legal 
punishments (hudud) when a young boy or girl, who had not yet reached maturity, was 
brought to him.  
Yet, He {a.s} would not nullify any of Allah’s {SWT} prescribed punishments." 
Imam Al Sadiq {a.s} was asked: “How would he strike with part of the whip?" 
Imam {a.s} replied: “ Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} would hold the whip by its middle and 
strike with it, or he would hold it by a third and strike with it, in proportion to their ages.  
In this way, he would strike them with the whip without nullifying any of Allah’s {SWT} 
prescribed punishments, the Mighty and Majestic." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Muhasin, Vol. 1 p.273 • Al -Kafi, Vol.7 p.176 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 4 p.74 • 
Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.146 • Fiqh Al-Quran, Vol.2 p.366 • Awali Al-La’ali, Vol.3 p.549 • Al-Wafi, Vol.15 
p.207 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.28 p.11 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.76 p.88  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 16, H. 5148

hadiths.chapter: 16. CHAPTER ON RARE CASES OF LEGAL PUNISHMENTS
hadiths.en:
Commander of the Faithful {a.s} delivered a sermon to the people and said: 
"Indeed, Allah {SWT}, the Blessed and Exalted, has set boundaries, so do not transgress 
them. He {SWT} has made obligations, so do not neglect them.  
And He  {SWT} has remained silent on certain matters - not out of forgetfulness - so do 
not burden yourselves with them.  
This is a mercy from Allah {SWT} for you, so accept it." 
Then Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"What is lawful ( halal) is clear, and what is unlawful ( haram) is clear, and between 
them are doubtful matters.  
Whoever avoids what is doubtful regarding sin will be even more cautious in avoiding 
what is clearly sinful.  
The acts of disobedience are the sanctuary of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic. 
Whoever grazes near it is likely to fall into it." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.75 • Al-Wafi, Vol.1 p.197 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.27 p.175  
BOOK ON BLOOD MONEY (DIYYAT) 109 
BOOK ON BLOOD MONEY (DIYYAT)

hadiths.chapter: 17. CHAPTER ON BLOOD MONEY FOR THE LIMBS AND JOINTS, AND BLOOD MONEY FOR THE NUTFAH (SPERM), AL
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Fadhal narrated from Zharif ibn Nasih from Abdullah ibn 
Ayyub, who said Al-Husayn Al-Rawwasi narrated to me from Ibn Abi U mayr, the 
physician, who said: I presented this narration to Abu Abdullah {a.s}, and Imam{a.s} said:  
“Yes, it is true. Indeed, the Commander of the Faithful {a.s} used to instruct his officials 
with this."  
He said: “Imam {a.s} gave a ruling that for every bone that contains marrow, there is a 
specified obligatory compensation if it is broken and healed without any defect or flaw.  
Imam {a.s} divided the obligatory blood money (diyyah) into six parts and established for 
wounds, the fetus, the eyelashes, paralysis, limbs, and the thumb, assigning six 
categories for each.

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 17, H. 5150

hadiths.chapter: 18. CHAPTER ON PROHIBITION OF SHEDDING BLOOD
hadiths.en:
Zurah narrated from Samaah from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
"Indeed, the Messenger of Allah {SWT} stood at Mina after completing his rituals during 
the Farewell Pilgrimage and said:  
‘O’ people, listen to what I {saws} say to you and understand it, for I {saws} do not know, 
perhaps I {saws} will not meet you again in this place after this year.'  
Then Prophet {saws} said: ‘Which day is the most sacred?'  
They replied: ‘This day.'  
Prophet {saws} said: ‘Which month is the most sacred?'  
They replied: ‘This month.'  
Prophet {saws} said: ‘Which land is the most sacred?'  
They replied: ‘This land.'  
Prophet {saws} then said:  
‘Indeed, your blood and your wealth are sacred upon you, just as the sanctity of this 
day of yours in this month of yours in this land of yours, until the day you meet Him 
{SWT}, and He {SWT} will question you about your deeds. Behold! Have I {saws} conveyed?'  
They replied: ‘Yes.'  
Prophet {saws} said:  
‘O’ Allah {SWT}, bear witness. Indeed, whoever has a trust in his possession, let him 
return it to the one who entrusted him with it, for it is not permissible for anyone to

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 18, H. 5151

hadiths.chapter: 18. CHAPTER ON PROHIBITION OF SHEDDING BLOOD AND TAKING WEALTH UNLAWFULLY, THE PROHIBITION OF E
hadiths.ar:
5153  -  وَّ رَّوَّى هِس َّامُّ ن ْن ُّ سَّالِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال ن ْ ذِي ن ِهِ مَّا لَّمْ  
ي ُّصِث ْ ذَّماً جَّزَّإماً
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Mansur Buzurj from Abu Hamzah 
Al-Thumali from Imam Ali ibn Al -Hussain {a.s}, who said: ‘The Messenger of Allah {SWT} 
said:  
“Do not be deceived by one who freely sheds blood, for indeed, he has a killer 
appointed for him by Allah {SWT} who does not die.” 
They asked: “O’ Messenger of Allah {SWT}, and what is the killer that does not die?”  
Prophet {saws} said: “The Fire." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.272 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.93 • Ma’ani Al-Akhbar, Vol.1 p.264 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.566 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.11 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.101 p.373  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 18, H. 5152

hadiths.chapter: 18. CHAPTER ON PROHIBITION OF SHEDDING BLOOD AND TAKING WEALTH UNLAWFULLY, THE PROHIBITION OF E
hadiths.en:
Hisham ibn Salim narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
“A believer remains within the bounds of his faith as long as he does not commit 
unlawful killing.”  
Imam {a.s} also said: “One who intentionally kills a believer is not granted success in 
repentance.” 
[REFERENCES] Tafsir Al-’Ayyashi, Vol.1 p.267 • Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.272 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.93 
• Al -Wafi, Vol.16 p.566 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.13 • Tafsir Al -Burhan, Vol.2 p.153 • Bihar Al -Anwar, 
Vol.101 p.378 • Tafsir Nur Al -Thaqalayn, Vol.1 p.533 • Tafsir Kanz Al -Daqaiq, Vol.3 p.507 • Mustadrak Al-
Wasa’il, Vol.18 p.205

hadiths.chapter: 18. CHAPTER ON PROHIBITION OF SHEDDING BLOOD AND TAKING WEALTH UNLAWFULLY, THE PROHIBITION OF E
hadiths.ar: 5155 - وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ إَّلْعَّلاَّءِ عَّن ِ إَّلي  ُّمَّالِى ِّ ق َّال  مِن َّ إَّلن  َّارِ
hadiths.en:
Hammad ibn Uthman narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
"On the Day of Judgment, a man will approach another man and smear him with blood 
while the people are being held accountable.  
The (other) man will say, 'O’ servant of Allah {SWT}, what do I have to do with you?'  
The man will reply: 'Did you not assist in my killing on such and such a day with a single 
word, and thus I was killed?'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.93 • Thawab Al -A’mal wa Iqab Al -A’mal, Vol.1 p.277 • 
Awali Al-La’ali, Vol.3 p.578 • Al -Wafi, Vol.16 p.568 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.12 p.304 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, 
Vol.29 p.18 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.7 p.217 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.101 p.383  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 18, H. 5154

hadiths.chapter: 18. CHAPTER ON PROHIBITION OF SHEDDING BLOOD AND TAKING WEALTH UNLAWFULLY, THE PROHIBITION OF E
hadiths.ar:
5156   -  رَّوَّى ج َّمِي ل  عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال هِ مَّن ْ إ َّجْذَّب َّ ن ِالْمَّذِي ن َّه ِ 
جَّذَّي اً إ َّوْ إ وَّى مُّحْذِي اً،
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Al-Alaa from Al-Thumali, that Imam {a.s} said:  
"If a man were to strike another man with a whip, Allah {SWT} would strike him with a 
whip of fire." 
[REFERENCES] Da’a’im Al-Islam, Vol.2 p.541 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.93 • Man La Yahduruhu 
Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.170 • Al-Wafi, Vol.5 p.964 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.22 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.22 
• Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.9 p.148 • Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.18 p.216  
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 18. CHAPTER ON PROHIBITION OF SHEDDING BLOOD AND TAKING WEALTH UNLAWFULLY, THE PROHIBITION OF E
hadiths.en:
Jamil narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
"The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace be upon him and his family) cursed the one who 
commits an offense in Medina or shelters an offender."  
I asked: “What is this offense?"  
Imam {a.s} said: “It is killing." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.275 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.93 • Ma’ani Al-Akhbar, Vol.1 p.264 
• Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.10 p.216 • Al-Wafi, Vol.14 p.1399 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.573 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 
p.22 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.26 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.27 p.66 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.101 p.373

hadiths.chapter: 18. CHAPTER ON PROHIBITION OF SHEDDING BLOOD AND TAKING WEALTH UNLAWFULLY, THE PROHIBITION OF E
hadiths.ar:
5158   -  رَّوَّى إ َّن َّان   عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إ ِسْحَّاق َّ إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ إَّلص َّي ْق َّلِ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ لِى  إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم ج ِذَّ ق ِى  ذ ُّو َّإن َّه ِ  
سَّي ْف ِ رَّسُّولِ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ صَّحِي ق َّه   ق َّا ِذ َّإ ق ِي هَّا مَّكْي ُّوب هِ إَّلز َّجْمٰن ِ إَّلز َّجِي مِ 
عَّلَّى إَّلل َّهِ ي َّوْمَّ إَّلْق ِي َّامَّه ِ مَّن ْ ق َّي َّلَّ ع َّي ْزَّ ق َّاي ِلِهِ وَّ ص َّزَّب َّ ع َّي ْزَّ ص َّارِن ِهِ وَّ مَّن ْ ي َّوَّل َّى ع َّي ْزَّ مَّوَّإلِي هِ  ق َّهُّوَّ كَّاق ِز  ن ِمَّا إ َّن ْز َّلَّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّى 
مُّحَّم َّذٍ وَّ مَّن ْ إ َّجْذَّب َّ جَّذَّ ي اً إ َّوْ إ وَّى مُّحْذِي اً لَّمْ ي َّق ْن َّلِ إَّلل َّهُّ ي َّعَّالَّى مِن ْهُّ، ي َّوْمَّ إَّلْق ِي َّامَّه ِ صَّزْق اً وَّ لأَّ عَّذْلأً ال 
ي َّذْرِى  مَّا ي َّعْن ِى  ن ِق َّوْلِهِ مَّن ْ ي َّوَّل َّى ع َّي ْزَّ مَّوَّإلِي هِ  
وَّ إلص َّزْف ُّ  إلي  َّوْن َّه ُّ ق ِى  ق َّوْلِ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ ع وَّ إلْعَّذْلُّ إلْق ِذَّإءُّ ق ِى  ق َّوْلِ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ.
hadiths.en:
Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from more than one person from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, 
who said: "Whoever assists in harming a believer with even half a word will come on 
the Day of Judgment with the words 'Despairing of the mercy of Allah {SWT}' written 
between his eyes." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.4 p.94 • Thawab Al -A’mal wa Iqab Al -A’mal, Vol.1 p.276 • 
Awali Al-La’ali, Vol.1 p.365 • Awali Al-La’ali, Vol.3 p.577 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.101 p.383  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 18, H. 5157

hadiths.chapter: 18. CHAPTER ON PROHIBITION OF SHEDDING BLOOD AND TAKING WEALTH UNLAWFULLY, THE PROHIBITION OF E
hadiths.en:
Aban narrated from Abu Ishaq Ibrahim Al-Sayqal, who said:  
Abu Abdullah {a.s} said to me: "A scroll was found in the hilt of the sword of Messenger 
of Allah {SWT} (peace be upon him and his family), and it was written in it:  
In the Name of Allah {SWT}, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.  
Verily, the most defiant of people against Allah {SWT} on the Day of Judgment is the one 
who kills someone other than his killer, strikes someone other than the one who struck 
him, and whoever aligns himself with other than his rightful guardians is a disbeliever 
in what Allah {SWT} revealed to Muhammad {saws}.  
And whoever commits an offense or shelters an offender, Allah {SWT} the Exalted will not 
accept from him any repentance or compensation on the Day of Judgment."  
Imam {a.s} then said: “Do you know what is meant by the saying 'whoever aligns himself 
with other than his rightful guardians'?"  
I asked: “What does it mean?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: “It means the people of the religion." 
[AL SADUQ] 
Al-Ṣarf means repentance, according to Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir 
{a.s}, and Al-Adl means ransom, according to Abu Abdullah {a.s}. 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.274 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.94 • Ma’ani Al-Akhbar, Vol.1 p.379 
• Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.74 p.125

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 18, H. 5158

hadiths.chapter: 18. CHAPTER ON PROHIBITION OF SHEDDING BLOOD AND TAKING WEALTH UNLAWFULLY, THE PROHIBITION OF E
hadiths.ar:
5160  -  وَّ رُّوِى مِي عاً لَّكَّان َّ إ ِن  َّمَّا  
ي َّذْج ُّلُّ ذ َّلِك َّ إَّلْمَّكَّان
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Hanan ibn Sadir from Abu Abdullah {a.s} regarding the saying 
of Allah {SWT}, the Almighty and Majestic:  
"Whoever kills a soul without [the soul having killed] another soul or [without causing] 
corruption on the earth, it is as if he had killed all mankind "  
(Surah Al-Ma'idah 5:32).  
Imam {a.s} said: “It is a valley in Hell; if a person were to kill all of mankind, he would be 
in it, and if he were to kill just one soul, he would still be in it."  
[REFERENCES] Tafsir Al-’Ayyashi, Vol.1 p.313 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.94 • Al-Wafi, Vol.16 p.565 
• Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.13 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, Vol.2 p.283 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.101 p.380 • Tafsir Nur 
Al-Thaqalayn, Vol.1 p.618 • Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.18 p.205  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 18, H. 5159

hadiths.chapter: 18. CHAPTER ON PROHIBITION OF SHEDDING BLOOD AND TAKING WEALTH UNLAWFULLY, THE PROHIBITION OF E
hadiths.ar:
5161  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْعَّلاَّءُّ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّجَّذِهِمَّا عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال ى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ 
إ لِهِالَّ وَّ لَّمْ إ ُّق َّاي ِلْ
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated:  
“A person who commits murder will be placed in a specific location in Hell, where the 
severity of punishment for its inhabitants reaches its peak. If he had killed all the 
people, he would only enter that same place.” 
It was asked: “And if he killed another?"  
Imam {a.s} said: “His punishment would be doubled." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.94 • Tafsir Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Vol.4 p.95  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 18, H. 5160

hadiths.chapter: 18. CHAPTER ON PROHIBITION OF SHEDDING BLOOD AND TAKING WEALTH UNLAWFULLY, THE PROHIBITION OF E
hadiths.en:
Al-Alaa narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim from one of the two Imams 
(peace be upon them), who said:  
“The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace be upon him and his family) said:  
‘Whoever is killed defending his wealth is a martyr.’"  
Imam {a.s} also said: “If it were me, I would have abandoned the wealth and not fought." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.95

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 18, H. 5161

hadiths.chapter: 18. CHAPTER ON PROHIBITION OF SHEDDING BLOOD AND TAKING WEALTH UNLAWFULLY, THE PROHIBITION OF E
hadiths.ar:
5163  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  وَّلأ َّذٍ إَّلْحَّن  َّاطِ ق َّالَّ سَّمِعْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم   ي َّق ُّول 
ن َّق ْسَّهُّ مُّي َّعَّمِّذإً ق َّهُّوَّ ق ِى  ن َّارِ ج َّهَّن  َّمَّ ج َّالِذإً ق ِي هَّا
hadiths.en:
Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Muhassin ibn Ahmad from Isa Al -Dhaif, who 
said:  
I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s}: "A man has killed another man; what is his repentance?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: “He should surrender himself."  
I said: “He fears they will kill him."  
Imam {a.s} said: “Then he should give them the blood money (diyyah)."  
I said: “He fears they will find out."  
Imam {a.s} said: “Then he should marry a woman from among them."  
I said: “He fears she might reveal this to them."  
Imam {a.s} said: "Then he should take the blood money, divide it into small bundles, 
observe the times of prayer, and secretly place it in their home."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.4 p.95 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.29 p.74  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 4, Ch. 18, H. 5162
